  "findings": [
    "Backfill lifts are too thick for the compaction equipment to achieve the required density.",
    "Non-compliance with project specifications and IPC Section 306 regarding proper backfilling and compaction."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop backfilling operations immediately.",
    "Excavate the improperly compacted backfill layers down to the compliant bedding level.",
    "Re-lay and compact the backfill in thinner, compliant lifts (typically 6 to 8 inches loose) using the appropriate compaction equipment.",
    "Perform field density testing to verify compaction meets project specifications before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "The backfill lifts exceed the maximum thickness that the contractor's compaction equipment can effectively consolidate. This leads to inadequate soil density, risking future settlement, pipe deflection, or structural failure of the utility line. This violates both the project specifications and IPC 306 requirements for proper pipe support and backfilling.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 306 (Trenching, Excavation and Backfill)",
    "Project Specifications (Sitework & Utilities - Earthwork/Backfill section)"
  ]
}
